name: CrowdSec
description: CrowdSec is an open-source, lightweight, and collaborative intrusion detection/prevention system that detects malicious behavior and can block IPs. It analyzes logs, shares threat intelligence, and works with bouncers to protect your infrastructure.

paths.name: Config
paths.description: CrowdSec configuration files, parsers, scenarios, and collections.

paths.name: Data
paths.description: CrowdSec database, local API decisions, and crowdsec-cli data.

paths.name: Logs
paths.description: Host system logs for CrowdSec to analyze.

ports.name: LAPI
ports.description: CrowdSec Local API port for bouncers to query decisions.

variables.name: Collection
variables.description: Comma-separated list of CrowdSec collections to install on startup.

variables.name: Enroll Key
variables.description: Enrollment key from CrowdSec Console (optional, for dashboard integration).

variables.name: Enroll Instance Name
variables.description: Name for this instance in CrowdSec Console.

variables.name: Level
variables.description: Enable info level logging (set to true for debugging).

variables.name: No Agent
variables.description: Disable the log parser agent (only run LAPI). Set to true if this is a dedicated LAPI server.

variables.name: No API
variables.description: Disable the Local API (only run agent). Set to true if connecting to remote LAPI.
